home.hero_desc: Discover Kendo — the traditional Japanese martial art where you can strengthen your body, sharpen your focus, and cultivate your spirit. Our club in Hanoi has been active since 2019 under the guidance of a 5th Dan instructor. Join us and enjoy two free trial classes!
home.trial_title: Want to give it a try?
home.trial_text: We offer 2 completely free trial sessions for newcomers. Spots fill up fast every week — register today to save yours!
home.seo_title: Kendo Club in Hanoi — 2 Free Trial Sessions
home.seo_desc: Yuei Kendo Community — Japanese kendo club in Hanoi since 2019. 5th dan sensei, 2 training locations, 2 free trial sessions for beginners. Sign up now!

about.title: About Us
about.story_p1: "Yuei" (輸贏) is a Japanese word meaning "to win or lose". It captures the spirit of kendo we live by. When you step onto the floor, you fight with everything you have — win with strength or lose with no regrets.
about.story_p2: We chose the flame as our symbol to represent that fierce spirit. The Yuei flame stands for the passion of every member and their dedication to kendo. With its red color, we hope to spread the spirit of kendo and ignite a fire in everyone who trains with us.
about.instructor_competitions: Participated in national and international competitions

faq.title: FAQ
faq.a1_p1: Kendo is a Japanese martial art that uses bamboo swords (shinai) and protective armor (bogu), evolved from the traditional samurai sword art of Kenjutsu. (Wikipedia)
faq.a1_p2: Today, kendo is practiced widely across Japan and has spread to many countries worldwide. As of 2019, there are approximately 6 million kendoka around the globe.
faq.q2: Why should I train at Yuei Kendo Community?
faq.a2_intro: Yuei Kendo Community was founded in 2019 in Hanoi, bringing together a diverse group of practitioners of all ages and backgrounds. Here is why Yuei stands out:
faq.a2_b1: Direct instruction from Nguyễn Mạnh Hưng — Godan (5th dan), 14 years of kendo practice, 10+ years of teaching, with experience in national and international competitions.
faq.a2_b2: A training schedule tailored for busy students and working professionals.
faq.a2_b3: Spacious, modern facilities well-suited for kendo practice.
faq.a2_b4: A clear training roadmap with quarterly gradings for those who take kendo seriously.
faq.a2_b5: Regular extracurricular activities and community social events.
faq.a2_b6: Opportunities for talented members to compete in international tournaments.
faq.a2_b7: A welcoming, open environment — both complete beginners and experienced kendoka are embraced.
faq.a3_p1: Anyone aged 5 and above can practice kendo and enjoy its wide range of physical and mental benefits.
faq.q4: What are the benefits of kendo?
faq.a4_intro: Kendo training delivers comprehensive benefits for both body and mind:
faq.a4_b1: Improved physical fitness — endurance, agility, and full-body coordination.
faq.a4_b2: Stress relief and better mental well-being.
faq.a4_b3: Character development — patience, discipline, decisiveness, etiquette, humility, kindness...
faq.a4_b4: Practical knowledge and skills applicable to everyday life.
faq.a4_b5: A broader social network and new meaningful connections.
faq.a4_b6: A fulfilling hobby and passion that can be pursued long-term.
faq.a5_p1: To register, simply fill out our Google Form, pay the first month's membership fee, and show up at the training venue during practice hours.
faq.q6: Can I try a session first?
faq.a6_p1: Yuei Kendo Community welcomes you to try 2 free sessions before committing to membership.
faq.q7: What do I need to bring?
faq.a7_p1: For your first session, just wear comfortable workout clothes and bring water. Yuei Kendo Community will prepare the training equipment you need.
faq.a7_p2: Once you decide to join, we will help you order the necessary gear — including the training uniform (hakama, keikogi) and bamboo sword (shinai).
faq.q8: What does the training curriculum look like?
faq.a8_intro: Kendo at Yuei follows a distinctive curriculum unlike traditional kendo clubs. The core technique set is "New Kihon Kendo" — developed by Hưng sensei (Godan), modeled on elite high school and university kendo in Japan:
faq.a8_b1: Chudan no Kamae — balanced stance integrating feet, body, and posture with power drawn from the "body core" (a foundation as steady as a mountain).
faq.a8_b2: Ghost Walk Ashi — Yuei's signature footwork: light, flexible, and balanced between offense and defense.
faq.a8_b3: Ki-Ken-Tai-Ichi striking technique — centered on Yuei Fumikomi (channeling full body weight into every strike, breath aligned with the sword).
faq.a8_b4: Kendo Zanshin — "remaining spirit / sustained awareness" after each strike, a defining focus at Yuei, rooted in top-tier Japanese university kendo.
faq.a9_p1: You can choose to pay 500,000 VND per month or 1,500,000 – 2,000,000 VND per quarter, depending on the number of sessions you register for.
faq.q10: Where can I ask other questions?
faq.a10_p1: For any other questions, feel free to message or leave a comment on our social media channels — we will get back to you as soon as possible.
faq.seo_desc: Everything about kendo in Hanoi: what kendo is, who can practice, membership fees, what to bring, and how to sign up for 2 free trial sessions at Yuei Kendo Community.
